forest-ink.$description: Forest Ink — Primary text, icons, and chart strokes — the load-bearing dark color. Reads near-black but carries a cool green undertone that ties the monochrome system together

bone-white.$description: Bone White — Card surfaces, data point fills, and reverse text on dark blocks

sage-paper.$description: Sage Paper — Page canvas and large background fields. A green-tinted light gray that gives the whole surface its herbarium character

ash-gray.$description: Ash Gray — Alternate surface tone for nested blocks, inset panels, and subtle differentiation beneath cards

muted-sage.$description: Muted Sage — Soft fill washes, scatter plot halos, and decorative surface tints that sit behind darker data marks

lichen.$description: Lichen — Hairline borders, dotted gridlines, and chart axes. The structural line color when a neutral needs to be present without competing with text

slate-smoke.$description: Slate Smoke — Secondary metadata, icon strokes, and caption text where the body tone is too heavy

charcoal-bark.$description: Charcoal Bark — Deep text, heavy borders, and dark surface blocks. Slightly warm-neutral, used for the rare dark panel and strong dividers

moss.$description: Moss — The single filled action color — primary buttons, active state fills, and the rare chromatic surface. A soft, slightly desaturated mint that does not shout against the sage canvas

deep-fern.$description: Deep Fern — Green text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

pine.$description: Pine — Green text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

emerald.$description: Emerald — Alternate data-mark fill and emphasis backgrounds, used on scatter plot points and callout chips

indigo-accent.$description: Indigo Accent — A single chromatic exception — used on one special link or annotation to break the green-only system. Employ rarely; treat as a punctuation mark, not a palette member

denim.$description: Primary workhorse — body copy, headings, and UI text. Weight 500 is used for headings and emphasis; weight 400 carries all body. Tracking sits at a slightly open +0.012em at body sizes, making the face feel like a working tool rather than a display face.

muoto.$description: Compact UI sans for tags, labels, captions, axis tick text, and micro-copy. Tight negative tracking (-0.009 to -0.012em) at 11–13px makes it dense and instrument-like rather than friendly. Weight 500 is reserved for uppercase labels.

cinetype.$description: Decorative tracking-heavy display face used only on ALL-CAPS labels, axis titles, and stamp-like badges. Tracking swings from a tight -0.008em to an extreme +0.30em, which is the visual signature of the system — it produces the 'specimen label' feel on tags and section markers.
